original_hypothesis.title: VoterV3 voted Flag Stuck True When All Voted Gauges Are Killed, Blocking Token Transfers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When poke() or vote() is called and all previously voted gauges are killed, _vote() calls _reset() (clearing weights) but only calls IVotingEscrow(_ve).voting(_tokenId) when _usedWeight > 0. Since all gauges are dead, _usedWeight = 0 and voting() is not called. However, the voted flag remains true from the previous vote, preventing transfers and withdrawals until the user calls reset() in the next epoch.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The _vote function sets voted=true only when _usedWeight > 0, but doesn't set it to false when _usedWeight = 0. Since poke() doesn't have onlyNewEpoch modifier restrictions, this state can be reached within the current epoch.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (_usedWeight > 0) IVotingEscrow(_ve).voting(_tokenId); — voted flag not cleared when _usedWeight == 0

preconditions: User has active votes on gauges
poke() is called (e.g., via deposit_for or increase_amount)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: voted flag only set to true when _usedWeight > 0; abstain only called in explicit reset()
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: After poke with all dead gauges: usedWeights=0 but voted=true. Token cannot be transferred or withdrawn.

analysis_questions: Can this be triggered via deposit_for which calls poke when voted?
How long is the user locked (until next epoch's reset window)?
Can this be weaponized to prevent emergency withdrawals?
validation_requirements: Test: vote for gauge, kill gauge, call deposit_for, verify transfer blocked
Verify reset() in next epoch properly clears voted flag
Check if there's any other way to clear voted flag

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A governance actor kills all gauges that a target user has voted for. When poke is triggered (via deposit_for or increase_amount), the user's voted flag stays true but usedWeights becomes 0. The user cannot transfer or withdraw their veNFT until the next epoch when they can call reset().

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Temporary lock of user funds (one epoch duration)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Automatic recovery next epoch via reset()

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for gauge killing impact on voted flag consistency

validation_result.mechanism: voted flag not cleared when _usedWeight == 0 in _vote
validation_result.impact_summary: When poke() or vote() is called and all voted gauges are dead, the voted flag remains true while usedWeights becomes 0, temporarily blocking token transfers and withdrawals until the next epoch when reset() can be called.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis correctly identifies a state inconsistency in VoterV3._vote(). When _vote() is called (via poke() or vote()) and all previously-voted gauges have been killed, _reset() clears the vote weight storage but does NOT call abstain(). The subsequent voting loop skips all dead gauges, leaving _usedWeight == 0. The conditional `if (_usedWeight > 0) IVotingEscrow(_ve).voting(_tokenId)` means voting() is not called. Critically, there is no corresponding `else { IVotingEscrow(_ve).abstain(_tokenId); }` branch, so the voted[_tokenId] flag from the previous successful vote persists as true. This blocks _transferFrom() and withdraw() in VotingEscrow, which both require `!voted[_tokenId]`. The user must wait until the next epoch to call reset() which does call abstain().
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code evidence directly supports the hypothesis. VoterV3._vote() only calls voting() when _usedWeight > 0 and never calls abstain(). VoterV3._reset() (internal) does not call abstain() - only the public reset() function does. VotingEscrow._transferFrom() and withdraw() both check `!voted[_tokenId]`. The proof_of_signal is accurate.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When all gauges are killed, _usedWeight remains 0. voting() is never called, and abstain() is never called either. The voted flag from the previous epoch's vote persists as true, creating an inconsistent state where usedWeights[_tokenId]==0 but voted[_tokenId]==true.

The internal _reset() function clears vote weights and pool data but does NOT call IVotingEscrow.abstain(). Only the public reset() function calls abstain() after _reset(). When _reset() is called from _vote(), the voted flag is left unchanged.
Transfer is blocked when voted[_tokenId] is true, which is the stuck state after poke() with all dead gauges.

poke() can be triggered via VotingEscrow.deposit_for() (msg.sender == _ve), which calls _vote() without any epoch restriction. It does not update lastVoted, but the _vote() path leaves voted=true when all gauges are dead.

calling_sequence: 1. User previously called vote() which set voted[_tokenId]=true and lastVoted[_tokenId]
2. All voted gauges are killed by governance
4. deposit_for checks voted[_tokenId] == true, calls IVoter(voter).poke(_tokenId)
5. poke() reads poolVote[_tokenId] (old pools) and votes[_tokenId][pool] (old weights)
7. _vote() calls _reset(_tokenId) - clears weights, poolVote, usedWeights but NOT voted flag
9. _usedWeight == 0 -> voting() NOT called -> voted[_tokenId] remains true
10. usedWeights[_tokenId] = 0 but voted[_tokenId] = true -> inconsistent state
11. User cannot transfer or withdraw until calling reset() in next epoch
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path from deposit_for -> poke -> _vote -> _reset creates inconsistency. _reset clears vote accounting without clearing the voted flag. _vote only sets voted=true (via voting()) when _usedWeight>0, and never sets voted=false (via abstain()). The only way to clear voted is through the public reset() function which has onlyNewEpoch guard. With WEEK=1800 (30 minutes), the user is locked for up to 30 minutes.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that contradict the hypothesis. The WEEK=1800 value in BlackTimeLibrary is hardcoded.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation:
The scenario is valid: (1) User votes for gauges in epoch N. voted=true, lastVoted set. (2) All voted gauges are killed by governance. (3) Anyone calls deposit_for() for the user's token, which triggers poke() since voted==true. (4) poke->_vote: _reset clears weights, all dead gauges => _usedWeight=0, voting() not called, abstain() not called. (5) voted[_tokenId]=true persists. usedWeights[_tokenId]=0. (6) User cannot call reset() until next epoch (blocked by onlyNewEpoch since lastVoted was set in current epoch). (7) Transfers and withdrawals blocked by require(!voted[_tokenId]).

The attack can also occur naturally without malicious intent - if gauges are killed as part of normal governance and deposit_for is called.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Medium severity. The user's veNFT is temporarily locked (cannot transfer or withdraw) for up to one epoch duration (1800 seconds / 30 minutes with current WEEK setting). This is a denial of service that blocks legitimate token operations. While recovery is possible in the next epoch via reset(), the user may not understand why their token is stuck. In a production setting with longer WEEK values, the impact could be more significant. Additionally, this can be weaponized by a governance actor who kills gauges to temporarily lock specific users' veNFTs.
recommended_mitigations: Add an else branch in _vote(): `if (_usedWeight > 0) IVotingEscrow(_ve).voting(_tokenId); else IVotingEscrow(_ve).abstain(_tokenId);`
Alternatively, call abstain() in the internal _reset() function rather than only in the public reset()
Add a check at the start of _vote() to save the previous voted state and ensure consistency on exit
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy VotingEscrow, VoterV3, GaugeManager, and supporting contracts. 2. Create a veNFT with create_lock. 3. Create a gauge for a pool. 4. Call vote() to vote for the pool. Verify voted[tokenId]=true. 5. Kill the gauge via GaugeManager. 6. Call deposit_for() to trigger poke(). 7. Verify voted[tokenId]=true and usedWeights[tokenId]=0. 8. Attempt transferFrom() - should revert with 'ATT'. 9. Advance time to next epoch, call reset(), verify voted=false and transfer works.
uncertainties: The exact behavior of gaugeManager.isGaugeAliveForPool() when a gauge is killed is via an interface call - the implementation is not in scope, but the hypothesis assumes it returns false for killed gauges, which is standard
Whether deposit_for can be called by anyone for any tokenId (it appears so from the code - no auth check on caller, only on token validity)
additional_testing_needed: Test with vote() instead of poke() - vote() in a new epoch with all dead gauges should have the same issue since _vote() is called
Test the exact epoch boundary timing to confirm when reset() becomes callable
Verify that increase_amount() also triggers the same path via poke()
Test with lockPermanent() and merge() which also call poke()
